steps.title: Identify like terms
steps.content: The terms $16x^2$ and $6x^2$ are like terms because they have the same variable part, $x^2$. The constant $5$ has no like term and stays separate.

steps.title: Combine the coefficients
steps.content:
Add only the coefficients of the $x^2$ terms; the exponent stays $2$:
$$16x^2+6x^2=22x^2$$
So the equivalent expression is
$$22x^2+5$$

steps.title: Match the expression
steps.content: Choice A changes the exponent to $4$, and choices C and D also miss the constant term. Choice B matches $22x^2+5$. The answer is B.
